title: The Seven Beer Snitch

jokes.text: They have a store that just sells shoes. I feel like I'm in Paris!
jokes.setup: Family is impressed by Shelbyville's sophistication
jokes.punchline: Marge thinks a shoe store is Parisian sophistication
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge naivety - her low bar for worldliness is endearing and character-specific

jokes.text: All plays suck, all the time, and always will, and everyone knows it!
jokes.setup: Lisa wants to see a play
jokes.punchline: Homer's absolute, universal condemnation of theater
jokes.explanation: Homer's aggressive philistinism delivered with complete confidence - perfectly in character

jokes.text: We'll go see a play... that Lisa doesn't want to see.
jokes.punchline: The 'compromise' is specifically to spite Lisa
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - malicious compliance disguised as fairness

jokes.text: the Three Dollar Bills in Gay, Gay, Gay!
jokes.punchline: Play title is both a currency pun and repetitive gayness
jokes.explanation: Mild pun with dated gay humor - more silly than clever

jokes.text: We're home to Christian, Muslim and Jew / Although not many of the last two.
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission of antisemitism and islamophobia
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of small-town prejudice hidden in cheerful song format - well-structured setup/payoff

jokes.text: There's a girl for every guy / Birthplace of the button fly
jokes.punchline: Random historical claim about trouser innovation
jokes.explanation: Absurd boast works through unexpected specificity of button fly claim

jokes.text: The only thing lower than our humidity are the greens fees on our public golf course. Which one? - All three!
jokes.punchline: They have three public courses, escalating the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - the enthusiasm for municipal bragging is funny

jokes.text: I came here for the legalized gambling. But Springfield Billy, we don't have legalized gambling here. Sure you do. I saw a bumper sticker that said 'Free Tibet.'
jokes.punchline: He thinks 'Free Tibet' means free gambling in Tibet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - takes political slogan and gives it hilariously literal gambling interpretation

jokes.text: They're saying Springfielders are stupid hicks. It's like lookin' in a mirror. What's a mirror? It's a big city word for reversifyin' glass.
jokes.setup: Springfielders offended at being called stupid
jokes.punchline: Immediately prove it by not knowing 'mirror' and making up 'reversifyin'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure - denying stupidity while demonstrating it. 'Reversifyin' glass' is quotable gold

jokes.text: This is the worst crisis the Springfield Cultural Activities Board has ever faced.
jokes.setup: Response to being mocked in a show
jokes.punchline: Treating entertainment mockery as existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - the self-importance of small-town cultural boards

jokes.text: show them we're more than just a town that's still afraid of eclipses. God steals sun : Mayor offers sacrifices
jokes.punchline: Newspaper reveals they literally think eclipses are divine theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual/textual gag - the juxtaposition of denial with evidence is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Hey, how 'bout we open a fancy restaurant, and when people check their hats and coats, we steal 'em?
jokes.setup: Need ideas to improve Springfield's image
jokes.punchline: Suggests elaborate theft scheme as cultural improvement
jokes.explanation: Character-specific criminal thinking applied to cultural problem - good misdirection

jokes.text: Why do you come to these meetings? Free water.
jokes.punchline: Purely motivated by complimentary refreshments
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words explain entire criminal mindset and civic engagement

jokes.text: Think, Marge, think. Culture... vulture... birds of prey... pray in a church... the Father, Son and Holy Ghost... ghosts are scary... scary rhymes with Gehry!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to think of cultural solutions
jokes.punchline: Bizarre word association chain leads to architect Frank Gehry
jokes.explanation: Delightfully absurd chain of associations - captures how random inspiration can feel logical in the moment

jokes.text: They're playing Gustav Mahler in abject squalor.
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description mixing high culture with degradation
jokes.explanation: Nice alliteration and cultural juxtaposition - classical music in terrible conditions

jokes.text: Little help? Next time we're keeping it.
jokes.setup: Musicians struggling with instruments in poor conditions
jokes.punchline: Defeated acceptance that their instruments are falling apart
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about poor conditions - functional but not memorable

jokes.text: we were the first city in America to abandon the metric system.
jokes.setup: Letter trying to impress Frank Gehry with Springfield accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Boasting about abandoning a measurement system they never adopted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - taking credit for something impossible and unnecessary

jokes.text: You wrote I was 'the bestest architect in the world'? Well, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Gehry quotes childish grammar from letter
jokes.punchline: Lisa sincerely defends her superlative usage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's earnestness overwhelming grammatical embarrassment

jokes.text: All in favor of building a $30 million 'screw you' to Shelbyville?
jokes.punchline: Framing massive municipal project as petty revenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect political language - dressing up petty motivation in civic terms

jokes.text: Yo, Frank Gehry! Like curvi-linear forms much?
jokes.punchline: Using architectural terminology as street insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant collision of subcultures - skater punk who knows architectural theory

jokes.text: Dad, Snowball's not fat. She's just got winter fur. Okay, she's fat.
jokes.punchline: Immediate surrender when she sees the cat
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Lisa's principles vs visual evidence

jokes.text: Can't breathe... Someone dangle some string.
jokes.punchline: Cat requests exercise motivation while immobilized
jokes.explanation: Solid pet obesity humor - cat retaining instincts despite incapacity

jokes.text: So? We already heard the 'dum dum dum dum.' The rest is just filler.
jokes.punchline: Reducing Beethoven's 5th to opening motif, rest is padding
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural satire - reducing classical masterwork to hook plus filler mentality

jokes.text: I thought this was gonna be the soundtrack to the movie Beethoven. Was I sadly mistaken.
jokes.punchline: Expected dog movie music, not the composer
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - low/high culture confusion with earnest disappointment

jokes.text: The next piece is an atonal medley by Philip Glass.
jokes.punchline: Offering even more inaccessible music as incentive
jokes.explanation: Good cultural gatekeeping satire - offering worse music to fleeing audience

jokes.text: We change the name of the town to escape our creditors. Lima, Peru! Gotham City! Wiggumville! Burger King!
jokes.punchline: Escalating from foreign city to corporate brand names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure from plausible to corporate absurdity

jokes.text: Make it seven!
jokes.setup: Burns notes Homer challenged him to fight six times during interview
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately adds another challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer impulsiveness - can't help escalating even when it's counterproductive

jokes.text: Cool, but I'll never pass with this. I better switch samples.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to pass drug test
jokes.punchline: Casually swaps his obviously contaminated sample
jokes.explanation: Standard drug test swap gag - functional but predictable

jokes.text: I haven't seen so many drugs in a wang since I ran a Chinese opium den!
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to personal history of drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - casually revealing dark past while examining urine

jokes.text: Crack, smack, uppers, downers, outters, inners, horse tranquilizers, cow paralyzers, blue bombers, green goofers, yellow submarines, LSD Mach 3
jokes.punchline: Escalating list mixing real and absurd drug names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with creative made-up drug names mixed with real ones

jokes.text: Well, there must have been a mistake. My body is a temple.
jokes.punchline: Claims purity after massive drug list
jokes.explanation: Standard irony - claiming innocence after obvious evidence of guilt

jokes.text: Guards, remove this coked-out man-balloon from my sight!
jokes.punchline: Describing Homer as inflated drug user
jokes.explanation: Creative Burns insult combining drug reference with Homer's physique

jokes.text: But he misread my pee! He misread my pee!
jokes.punchline: Desperate protest about urine interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good desperation comedy - treating drug test like misunderstood literature

jokes.text: I'd rather follow the dog. I think he's up to something.
jokes.setup: Family deciding to follow the cat
jokes.punchline: Bart suspects the dog of mysterious activities
jokes.explanation: Good Bart paranoia - suspicious of innocent family pet

jokes.text: This is worse than when we thought Mom was having an affair. Turned out she was just going to the library to cry.
jokes.punchline: Revealing Marge's secret depression was relief compared to affair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - casual revelation of family dysfunction wrapped in comparative relief

jokes.text: We need more convicts in the dungeonarium. Dungeonarium. That's catchy.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining dungeon with aquarium gets approval
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay creation with Wiggum's simple appreciation of corporate speak

jokes.text: It's unlawful for a man to be hatless during daylight hours.
jokes.punchline: Absurd 19th century hat requirement
jokes.explanation: Standard old law humor - absurd but historically plausible regulation

jokes.text: Five kicks of the same can shall be considered illegally transporting litter.
jokes.punchline: Specific threshold for can-kicking becoming criminal
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in absurd law - the exact number makes it funnier

jokes.text: We're puttin' you away, Kicky Mantle.
jokes.punchline: Nickname combining kicking with baseball legend Mickey Mantle
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau nickname - Wiggum creating police persona for petty criminal

jokes.text: And give that can the whole CSI treatment. You know, lab tests, weird lights, shots from the can's point of view.
jokes.setup: Police treating can like crime scene evidence
jokes.punchline: Wiggum knows TV crime show conventions and wants to copy them
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about TV crime procedural clichés

jokes.text: Lots of flash and no meaning.
jokes.punchline: Accurately describing modern crime show aesthetics
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in media criticism - distills entire genre in four words

jokes.text: I'm sick of you positing an alternate reality!
jokes.setup: Homer insisting he should have guard's job
jokes.punchline: Guard using academic language to describe Homer's delusions
jokes.explanation: Good vocabulary humor - prison guard speaking like philosophy professor

jokes.text: No Frank Gehry-designed prison can hold me!
jokes.punchline: Crediting architectural style as escape motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier Gehry plot - prisoner treating architecture as personal affront

jokes.text: Really? You mean eat cheese, gnaw through electrical cords, things like that?
jokes.punchline: Homer literally interpreting rat behavior
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation humor - Homer missing criminal slang

jokes.text: Well, I am a sucker for a little hat.
jokes.setup: Burns offering perks for being a snitch
jokes.punchline: Homer swayed by trivial accessory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - abandoning principles for cute accessories

jokes.text: Well, I would like to survive long enough to see the effects of global warming. I've got an inside tip that it's all a bunch of crap.
jokes.punchline: Wanting to live to witness climate change denial being proved right
jokes.explanation: Good climate change skepticism humor with Homer's inside source delusions

jokes.text: Sometimes I go to movies alone.
jokes.setup: Burns wants to hear something he doesn't know
jokes.punchline: Tough mob boss reveals vulnerable personal detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor - mob boss revealing relatable loneliness in perfect economy

jokes.text: G.M.S.: Galactic Mystery Solvers. The only mystery we couldn't solve was how not to grow up.
jokes.punchline: Childhood club's unsolved mystery is arrested development
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup/payoff - childhood club concept leading to poignant life failure observation

jokes.text: Solitary confinement? But without my weekly canasta, I'll die. It's-it's my raison d'être.
jokes.punchline: Prison card game as existential purpose with French philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good pretentious prisoner humor - treating card game like high art

jokes.text: And for your information, our walls have a bunch of dead raccoons in them, but we are saving up the money to find them.
jokes.punchline: Knowing about dead animals but treating removal as luxury expense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist domestic logic - treating corpse removal as budgetary consideration

jokes.text: C'mon, Smokey, do your Smokey shuffle.
jokes.punchline: Cat apparently has signature dance move
jokes.explanation: Simple pet trick humor - cat having performance routine

jokes.text: You toyed with my heart like it was a toy heart.
jokes.punchline: Redundant metaphor about heart being toy-like
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa melodrama - her tendency toward overwrought emotional expression

jokes.text: we must determine which of our fellow inmates has become the Rodentus Incarcerarium.
jokes.punchline: Mob boss using mock-Latin scientific classification for 'rat'
jokes.explanation: Excellent educated mob boss humor - creating fake Latin taxonomy for criminal slang

jokes.text: I ain't the rat; I'm the pigeon. I thought you was the mole. No, you're thinking of that guy who was the canary. But we can all agree, we work in a business with a very rich lexicon.
jokes.setup: Prisoners trying to identify the snitch
jokes.punchline: Cataloging all animal-based criminal informant terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-linguistic humor - prisoners appreciating their criminal vocabulary richness

jokes.text: Rich lexicon, very rich.
jokes.punchline: Simple affirmation of vocabulary appreciation
jokes.explanation: Simple callback emphasizing prisoners' intellectual pride in their terminology

jokes.text: Sure, they're on top of the world. Then there's a big riot scene. Then... I don't know. I usually doze off.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about prison movie fates
jokes.punchline: Homer loses interest in movies before seeing consequences
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization - his attention span preventing him from learning consequences

jokes.text: What are you dopes looking at? You've never seen bling-bling before?
jokes.punchline: Homer using dated slang while oblivious to danger
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer obliviousness with period slang usage

jokes.text: My baby turtle crawled into your basement window. Oh, you poor boy. What's your turtle's name? Apron Boobs-face?
jokes.setup: Bart using fake story to investigate house
jokes.punchline: Bart's improvised pet name is ridiculous and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good Bart improvisation humor - his terrible fake pet names under pressure

jokes.text: And what's your name? Shoes... Butt-back?
jokes.setup: Mother asking Bart's name after turtle name
jokes.punchline: Bart continues with equally absurd fake name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's bad improvisation skills

jokes.text: Are biographies arranged by author or subject?
jokes.setup: Burns wants inside information about jailbreak
jokes.punchline: Homer asks library science question instead
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Homer completely missing the context and asking practical library question

jokes.text: You forgot to take some brownies. Thanks, Mother Dexter!
jokes.punchline: Adopting maternal nickname from TV serial killer show
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference humor with maternal serial killer connection

jokes.text: Something don't feel right. Usually when there's a jailbreak, there's electricity in the air, like the calm before a summer storm. Or, right before a heavyweight fight. Mine's more poetic.
jokes.punchline: Guard comparing atmosphere to weather and claiming poetic superiority
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about guard thinking his simile is more artistic

jokes.text: I call the balls of his feet!
jokes.punchline: Prisoner claiming specific, unusual body part for violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about violence planning with oddly specific body part choice

jokes.text: I only did what I had to do to survive... in a style befitting a French monarch.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his snitching to Marge
jokes.punchline: Admitting his motivation was luxury rather than survival
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer rationalization - revealing his true shallow motivations while claiming noble ones

jokes.text: This red glow is the heat from a pack of inmates who are desperately looking for prison snitch, Homer Simpson-- this massive blob over here-- who is attempting to hide in the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Describing Homer as 'massive blob' on thermal imaging
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer body humor with thermal imaging technology

jokes.text: Oh, you must be Michael's dad!
jokes.punchline: Marge treating murderous inmate like PTA parent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge politeness - maintaining suburban social norms during prison riot

jokes.text: Tear gas, my one weakness.
jokes.punchline: Prisoner treating universal crowd control method like personal kryptonite
jokes.explanation: Good superhero parody - treating universal weakness like personal vulnerability

jokes.text: This prison will make Abu Ghraib look like the Four Seasons.
jokes.punchline: Comparing to notorious torture prison as baseline for improvement
jokes.explanation: Dark topical humor about prison abuse scandal - typical Burns evil

jokes.text: They feed us horse meat, with traces of jockey meat!
jokes.punchline: Escalating from horse meat to including the riders
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - taking bad prison food to absurd extreme

jokes.text: When I was a boy, I saw my father murdered before my eyes. By me.
jokes.setup: Guard explaining his sadistic nature with tragic backstory
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was the murderer, not traumatized witness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor setup/payoff - perfect misdirection from victim to perpetrator with economical delivery

jokes.text: I'm releasing you all to a garbage barge, where you will bare-knuckle-box till one of you emerges as king of your floating hell.
jokes.punchline: Creating floating gladiator combat solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist political solution - combining waste management with gladiator combat

jokes.text: No, Dad, you fell asleep in front of the TV, watching The Towering Inferno.
jokes.punchline: Revealing his 'prophetic vision' was just a disaster movie
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Homer's dramatic warning deflated by mundane explanation

tmdb_overview: After Shelbyville accuses Springfield of being hicks, Marge convinces the townspeople to fund a Frank Gehry-designed concert hall, which is then converted to a prison when the concert hall goes bankrupt on opening night. Meanwhile, Bart and Lisa investigate Snowball II's recent weight gain.
